title: Only 38% of Calls Get Answered — Here's What Happens to the Rest
hook_line: One study found that only 38 percent of calls to small businesses get answered by a real person.
cliffhanger_line: And the infrastructure underneath all of it? Just raised at an $11 billion valuation.
pinned_comment: Full breakdown of the business, the evidence, and the honest economics is in the full episode — link in bio.
why: Opens on the sourced 38/62 split, names the dollar range and the $11B figure to create curiosity, but withholds the actual mechanism (what gets installed, how) to force a click to the full video.

title: This Voice Reading This Sentence Is AI
hook_line: And look, you're actually listening to the proof right now.
cliffhanger_line: And exactly where the seams still show.
pinned_comment: The full stack, the seams, and where this breaks in real client calls — full episode linked.
why: Single self-contained beat with a strong meta-hook (the video itself is the proof). Ends by raising a specific unresolved tension — where exactly it breaks — without naming it, which only the full video answers.

title: The Math Behind a Missed Phone Call
hook_line: A plumber missing 15 calls a week at $350 a job? That's six figures a year left on the table.
cliffhanger_line: Because it matches their own missed-call screen.
pinned_comment: The real cost to build this (under $150/month) and the pricing model that works is broken down in the full video.
why: Delivers the emotionally sticky arithmetic and behavioral stats (85%/62%) as the 'first insight,' but explicitly poses the cost/pricing question without answering it, pushing viewers to the full breakdown for the stack and numbers.

title: The One Question That Decides If This Business Survives
hook_line: So the open question isn't whether small businesses will pay for this.
cliffhanger_line: Or whether ElevenLabs and Retell just... sell it themselves.
pinned_comment: The platform risk, the churn risk, and the honest year-one income range are all in the full episode.
why: Reframes the obvious question (will businesses pay) and introduces the sharper, unresolved one (does the agency layer survive), naming the threat (platforms going direct) but deliberately not resolving which way it goes — that resolution lives only in the full video and blueprint.
